purpose: Verify that recently implemented Ananta features are real, runnable and safely integrated by expanding unit, integration, contract, smoke and e2e coverage, with special focus on TUI and Eclipse plugin claims.
goal: Before merging feature-driven work to main, Ananta must distinguish real runnable features from foundation-only work and prove core behavior through automated tests, manual smoke checks and explicit release gates.
success_criteria: The test suite clearly verifies implemented behavior instead of only testing design helpers or static contracts.
TUI functionality is either proven runnable through tests/smoke checks or explicitly reclassified as foundation-only.
Eclipse plugin functionality is either proven as an actual plugin project or explicitly reclassified as adapter foundation-only.
Core flows have unit, integration and e2e/smoke coverage for success, failure and degraded states.
Merge readiness is based on executable evidence, not only completed todo status.
architecture_guardrails: Tests must not mark features as complete if no executable entrypoint or real integration exists.
Foundation, contract, documentation and real runnable implementation must be classified separately.
Safety, approval, audit and verification behavior must include negative tests.
TUI and Eclipse tests must avoid silently validating only backend placeholder services.

milestones.title: Core backend unit and contract coverage

tasks.title: Feature reality inventory create
acceptance_criteria: A machine-readable or documented inventory classifies recent features as real_implementation, foundation_only, docs_only, test_only or missing.
The inventory covers deterministic repair, instruction layers, tool routing, context manager, TUI, Neovim/Vim, Eclipse plugin and Eclipse extended views.
No feature may remain ambiguously marked as done without implementation evidence.

tasks.title: Executable entrypoint audit add
acceptance_criteria: A script or test checks for real executable entrypoints for TUI, CLI, plugin or service features where claimed.
The audit fails or warns when todo status claims a runnable feature but only foundation services or docs exist.
The audit output is suitable for release review.

tasks.title: Todo status versus implementation evidence check add
acceptance_criteria: A validation check compares done tasks against expected files, entrypoints, tests or docs evidence.
The check can flag likely false-positive done tasks such as Eclipse plugin done without plugin.xml or TUI done without a TUI entrypoint.
The check is documented and can be run locally before merge.

tasks.title: Foundation-only reclassification rules define
acceptance_criteria: Rules define when a feature must be called foundation-only instead of complete.
TUI, Eclipse plugin and editor integration tracks have explicit completion definitions.
The rules are added to release or contributor documentation.

tasks.title: Deterministic repair negative unit tests expand
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover unsafe action rejection, low-confidence handling, failed verification and abort conditions.
Tests verify that approval-required repair steps cannot execute without approval.
Tests verify that audit and outcome memory are updated for success and failure cases.

tasks.title: Approval policy unit tests expand
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover allow, deny, approval-required and capability-missing cases.
Tests verify fail-closed behavior for unknown or malformed actions.
Tests cover repair, task execution and IDE-originated operations.

tasks.title: Instruction layer and profile precedence tests add
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify stable precedence between system, user profile, task overlay and session instructions.
Tests verify that user/task overlays cannot override governance, approval or safety rules.
Tests cover invalid, missing and conflicting instruction-layer input.

tasks.title: Tool routing and doom-loop tests expand
acceptance_criteria: Tool routing tests cover allowed, denied, unknown and degraded backend cases.
Doom-loop tests cover repeated failed actions, repeated same-tool calls and correction-path triggering.
Tests verify that loop detection produces explicit state instead of silent continuation.

tasks.title: Context bundle and bounded context tests expand
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify context size limits, source prioritization and provenance tracking.
Tests cover editor/TUI/Eclipse-originated context packages.
Tests verify that secrets or unrelated project content are not included implicitly.

tasks.title: Goal to task to artifact integration test add
acceptance_criteria: An integration test covers Goal -> Plan -> Task -> Execution -> Verification -> Artifact at least with mocked/safe execution.
The test verifies state transitions and artifact creation.
The test verifies that audit/trace identifiers link the flow together.

tasks.title: Repair flow integration test add
acceptance_criteria: An integration test covers known signature -> diagnosis -> proposed repair -> approval gate -> execution -> verification -> memory update.
A second integration test covers unsafe/denied repair where execution must not happen.
The tests use safe fake commands or controlled test doubles.

tasks.title: API contract tests for external clients add
acceptance_criteria: Contract tests cover endpoints/read models used by Web UI, CLI, TUI and Eclipse plugin surfaces.
Tests verify stable fields for goals, tasks, artifacts, approvals, health, capabilities and traces.
Breaking API changes become visible before merge.

tasks.title: Degraded backend integration tests add
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover unreachable LLM backend, unavailable worker, failed tool call and unavailable storage/retrieval component where applicable.
The system returns explicit degraded states instead of crashing or hanging.
Client-facing read models remain understandable under degraded conditions.

tasks.title: Security and privacy regression tests add
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify token redaction in logs, traces and client error messages.
Tests cover context packaging with likely secret patterns.
Tests verify that restricted actions remain blocked from UI/client-originated requests.

tasks.title: TUI existence and entrypoint test add
acceptance_criteria: A test or audit verifies whether a real TUI package and executable entrypoint exists.
If no executable TUI exists, the track is explicitly marked foundation-only and not complete.
The check looks for a runnable command such as ananta tui or an equivalent module entrypoint.

tasks.title: TUI view model unit tests add
acceptance_criteria: TUI view models for task list, task detail, artifact list/detail, approvals, logs and health are unit tested where implemented.
Tests cover empty, normal, error and permission-denied states.
Tests avoid depending on terminal rendering when pure view models are enough.

tasks.title: TUI terminal smoke test add
acceptance_criteria: A smoke test starts the TUI in a non-interactive or scripted mode if implementation exists.
The smoke test verifies startup, backend connection handling and at least one rendered core view.
If scripted terminal automation is not practical, a documented manual smoke checklist is required.

tasks.title: TUI approval and repair safety tests add
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify that TUI approval actions call backend approval paths and do not bypass policy.
Tests verify that repair execution review stays explicit and dry-run-first where applicable.
Tests cover rejected, denied and stale approval states.

tasks.title: TUI documentation and runnable demo verify
acceptance_criteria: The TUI user guide includes exact commands to start and use the TUI if implemented.
A golden path demo covers connection, task inspection and artifact inspection.
The demo is either automated or manually smoke-tested and recorded as release evidence.

tasks.title: Eclipse plugin project existence test add
acceptance_criteria: A test or audit verifies whether a real Eclipse plugin project exists with plugin.xml, MANIFEST.MF or equivalent PDE/build metadata.
If no plugin project exists, Eclipse work is explicitly reclassified as adapter foundation only.
The audit output names the expected plugin project path.

tasks.title: Eclipse plugin build smoke test add
acceptance_criteria: If a real Eclipse plugin exists, CI or local script can build it with the chosen Eclipse/PDE/Tycho/Gradle setup.
The build verifies plugin metadata, dependencies and packaging.
If build automation is not ready, a documented manual build checklist is required and the feature is not marked fully release-ready.

tasks.title: Eclipse adapter service unit tests expand
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover connection profiles, health/capability handshake, context packaging and task/artifact parsing.
Tests cover failure cases such as auth failure, unreachable backend and malformed task response.
Tests verify that Eclipse-originated actions stay mapped to official Ananta flows.

tasks.title: Eclipse view model and synchronization tests add
acceptance_criteria: If Eclipse views exist, tests cover goal, task, artifact, context and review view models.
Tests verify selection synchronization and stale-state handling across views.
If views do not exist, the test task records that extended views are not implemented yet.

tasks.title: Eclipse manual e2e smoke checklist add
acceptance_criteria: A manual checklist covers installing/opening the plugin, configuring Ananta URL/token, sending selected code, creating a goal and viewing returned task/artifact state.
The checklist includes expected results and known failure symptoms.
The checklist is short enough to run before merging the branch.

tasks.title: Merge readiness test gate define
acceptance_criteria: A merge gate defines minimum required passing tests for main.
The gate explicitly separates blocking tests from advisory tests.
TUI and Eclipse claims must pass existence/build/smoke checks or be downgraded before merge.

tasks.title: CI or local test runner command consolidate
acceptance_criteria: There is one documented command or script to run the relevant test suite locally.
The command includes unit and integration tests and optionally skips manual/Eclipse-only smoke checks with clear reporting.
Test output is suitable for deciding whether the branch is merge-ready.

tasks.title: Golden path regression suite add
acceptance_criteria: A small regression suite covers the most important product paths: CLI goal, repair flow, approval flow, artifact flow, UI/client read model and degraded backend behavior.
The suite runs without requiring destructive local system changes.
The suite is documented as the minimum sanity check before release.

tasks.title: Post-test cleanup todo generate
acceptance_criteria: After tests are added, remaining gaps are written into a cleanup todo or issue list.
Gaps distinguish missing implementation, weak test coverage, documentation drift and future enhancements.
The cleanup list is suitable for deciding what can merge now and what must wait.

execution_order_summary: Stage 1: Reality audit first, so false-complete features are exposed before more code is added.
Stage 2: Expand backend unit and contract tests for safety-critical services.
Stage 3: Add integration and e2e tests for core product flows.
Stage 4: Verify TUI as runnable implementation or reclassify it as foundation-only.
Stage 5: Verify Eclipse plugin as real plugin project or reclassify it as foundation-only.
Stage 6: Define merge gate and produce cleanup todo from test evidence.
summary_notes: This track intentionally prioritizes verification over new features.
TUI and Eclipse must be proven runnable or explicitly downgraded to foundation-only.
The goal is to prevent false-positive done status before merging feature-driven work into main.
